title: Military Weapons vehicles in Eva
wikitext:
[]: Military Weapons/vehicles in Eva             Quote Post by Stryker » Sun Jul 10, 2011 8:24 pm Can we get a list of them? I'm working on sounds, and I need to know what weapons/vehicles are so I know what sounds I need.

[]:             Quote Post by SAWgunner » Sun Jul 10, 2011 8:35 pm Gendo and Fuyutski fly around in a CH-53 Super Stallion helicopter in ep. 16 or 17. Multiple Launch Rocket Systems shoot at Sachiel in episode 1, and you see a battalion of M-60 Patton tanks (or a Japanese equivalent from the same generation of AFVs)* lined up to fight aforesaid Angel at the start of episode 1. Hummvees get a lot of play in different parts of the series. I think Nerv guys drive around in Fuchs transport vehicles in Rebuild, but I'm not completely sure. Misato keeps a USP40 as her sidearm. All I can think of off the top of my head, I'll see if I come up with anything else. *The M-60 series became obsolete in the early nineties after Desert Storm. Why does Japan still have them in 2015?

[]:             Quote Post by symbv » Sun Jul 10, 2011 9:04 pm SAWgunner wrote: and you see a battalion of M-60 Patton tanks (or a Japanese equivalent from the same generation of AFVs)* lined up to fight aforesaid Angel at the start of episode 1. *The M-60 series became obsolete in the early nineties after Desert Storm. Why does Japan still have them in 2015?

[]:             Quote Post by soul.assassin » Sun Jul 10, 2011 9:53 pm The following might help (some captions identify the vehicles): Resources:Explanation of Evangelion 1.01 Resources:Explanation of Evangelion 1.01 (Pt. 1) Resources:Explanation of Evangelion 1.01 (Pt. 2)

[]:             Quote Post by Stryker » Sun Jul 10, 2011 10:00 pm So, here is what we mostly know: Vehicles CH-53 Super Stallion - UH sounds Type 76 MOD - M1 sounds Humvees - Humvee sounds VTOL Attack Vehicle - Talon sounds, Weapon Sounds: Reisig Sounds VTOL Gunship (featured in EoE) - Shepard sounds, Weapon Sounds - Talon Gunner Sounds Yak-38 Aircraft - Talon Sounds (highspeed) AMX-10 - Bradley Sounds Infantry Arms USP40 Sidearm - Grach sounds G11 Rifle - 416 sounds MPi-9 - M1A1 sounds CZ-75 - 1911 Sounds HPM 1 - Pierra Sounds Glock - M9 sounds Hand Cannon - Rex sounds flame Thrower - Flame thrower sounds Panzerfaust - Carl Sounds Eva weapons Hand Cannon - Rex sounds (deep) Pallet Gun - M8 sounds Hand Bazooka - RPG-7 sounds (tripled, deepened) Sniper Rifle - M95 sounds (Deepened) Positron Sniper Rifle - Rorsch sounds (deepened) I'm sure there are more then just that.

[]:             Quote Post by SAWgunner » Sun Jul 10, 2011 11:05 pm For Eva weapons, don't forget Shinji's city bus-size Desert Eagle.

[]:             Quote Post by Viking » Mon Jul 11, 2011 4:35 am Well, I'm not entirely sure, but in the episode eight there are also two IOWA-class battleships, a Nimitz-class aircraft carrier ( CVN-75 "Over the Rainbow" ), a Kuznetsov-class aircraft carrier ( maybe the Adm. Kuznetsov herself, the only thing I can remember is a rather large skyjump ), lot of Knaapo SU-33 "Flanker", a Yakovlev YAK-38 "Forger" and a lot of other ships I can't remember without watching ep. 8 again :) Bye EDIT: Ok, sorry, everything I wrote was already in the wiki .

[]:             Quote Post by Hyper Shinchan » Mon Jul 11, 2011 4:59 am This page can be useful for personal weapons, while this other one is useful for the vehicles. Anyway the later needs some clean-up and it's lacking some vehicles and some infos, for example there's no reference to the "Type-76 mod" mentioned by symbv in the Type 74 section. Actually I really forgot the Explanation of Evangelion (the silly video was subbed in the Italian BD of 1.11 but I never bored watching it), it'll be useful to source the remaining vehicles (together with the CRC 2.22 that I own); I'm going to finish working on it as soon as I can.

[]:             Quote Post by Stryker » Mon Jul 11, 2011 11:48 am I just need the original series. Thanks, ;)

[]:             Quote Post by Hyper Shinchan » Mon Jul 11, 2011 12:19 pm Stryker wrote: I just need the original series. Thanks, ;)

[]:             Quote Post by Stryker » Mon Jul 11, 2011 1:00 pm Well, it seems that the Nerv Sentinel seemed to be carrying an Uzi, not a MPi-9. Look here: SPOILER: Show

[]:             Quote Post by Hyper Shinchan » Wed Jul 13, 2011 3:04 pm Unfortunately I can't help with that, guns aren't between my interests. Anyway I have a question for symbv: can you confirm that the "Type 76 mod" was already called like that in NGE? Do you remember the source by chance (I'd like to mention it in the wiki)? It's effectively called like that in the "Explanation of Evangelion" but I never heard anything about it before you mentioned it (so I'm not sure if it applies to NGE as well, I think that it's the case but I'd like to have a direct confirmation).

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Wed Jul 13, 2011 4:15 pm Those instances of the wiki page were added by me under the advice of BobBQ, who I consider to be the local gun expert. Here is the log: [code:1]2010-09-28 23:11:20 <Ornette> actually, one sec, I need to find the link 2010-09-28 23:12:07 <Ornette> when in the TV series/movies was the a P90 submachine gun used? 2010-09-28 23:13:56 <Ornette> if you don't remember, that's fine 2010-09-28 23:16:40 <Eric_Blair> if it was ever used, it was in EoE 2010-09-28 23:16:46 <Eric_Blair> and I'm not sure it was used even there 2010-09-28 23:17:01 <Ornette> I went through a lot of the invasion scenes lookin for screenshots 2010-09-28 23:17:06 <Fuyutsuki> Her boots were maid for walking~ 2010-09-28 23:17:10 <Ornette> but I didn't think I saw a P90 2010-09-28 23:52:27 <BobBQ> There's an EoE concept sketch for a modified P90 (like the ones Jan Valentine uses in Helsing) but I don't remember ever seeing it on screen. 2010-09-28 23:52:40 <Ornette> ah, ok 2010-09-28 23:53:10 <Ornette> I saw that it was in the JSSDF article but couldn't find a screenshot of it to add to the guns article 2010-09-29 00:13:31 <BobBQ> Ornette: a few other points that should be fixed if you're going to tweak that weapons page. 2010-09-29 00:14:07 <BobBQ> 1: the SSDF Hi-Power is a 1940s Inglis model, built in Canada. 2010-09-29 00:14:23 <BobBQ> 2: according to Chronicle, Misato's USP is a .45 model. 2010-09-29 00:14:38 <BobBQ> 3: the G11 was developed in the 1970s-80s, not the 1960s as it currently says. 2010-09-29 00:15:19 <BobBQ> 4: Aoba's SMG is not a Steyr MPi 69, but a fictional mashup of the MPi and the Russian AEK-919. (Good images of it in Chronicle also) 2010-09-29 00:17:42 <BobBQ> To be extra-picky, the Hi-Power is an Inglis No 2 Mk 1, as manufactured for Allied forces and still used by the Canadian military. 2010-09-29 00:18:57 <Ornette> you happen to have an image handy of the Mk I? 2010-09-29 00:19:57 <Ornette> only image I can find is really crappy resolution 2010-09-29 00:22:57 <BobBQ> Ornette: http://www.imfdb.org/index.php/Image:Inglis_Hi-Power.JPG 2010-09-29 00:24:37 <Ornette> hmm, I found one of the same side 2010-09-29 00:26:05 <BobBQ> You sure? 2010-09-29 00:26:33 <BobBQ> The main giveaway that it's an Inglis and not an FN from the same period is the sight arrangement. 2010-09-29 00:26:42 <Ornette> not sure 2010-09-29 00:26:46 <BobBQ> Link? 2010-09-29 00:26:59 <Ornette> http://www.canadiansoldiers.com/weapons/holsters/398px-Inglis_R.jpg 2010-09-29 00:27:26 <Ornette> wnated one on this side so that it can easily be compared with the screenshot 2010-09-29 00:27:50 <BobBQ> Yeah, that's it. 2010-09-29 00:29:20 <BobBQ> For comparison, here's an FN from the same period: http://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/5/53/Browning_HP_1944.jpg 2010-09-29 00:29:33 <Ornette> ah 2010-09-29 00:32:17 <BobBQ> The large hump rear right and dovetailed front sight are unique to the Inglis No 2 and one Hi-Power clone currently made in India on ex-Inglis tooling.[/code:1] As for the Nerv people not carrying Stery MPi's, I can ask him to look at your pictures if I see him in the channel.

[]:             Quote Post by symbv » Wed Jul 13, 2011 9:37 pm Hyper Shinchan wrote: Anyway I have a question for symbv: can you confirm that the "Type 76 mod" was already called like that in NGE? Do you remember the source by chance (I'd like to mention it in the wiki)? It's effectively called like that in the "Explanation of Evangelion" but I never heard anything about it before you mentioned it (so I'm not sure if it applies to NGE as well, I think that it's the case but I'd like to have a direct confirmation).

[]:             Quote Post by Hyper Shinchan » Thu Jul 14, 2011 4:20 am Oh, between the links at the end there's our wiki, they've linked the page of "Explanation of Evangelion 1.01", I wonder if they've partially sourced them from here. The article apparently covers both NGE and NME, I guess that for now we could say that "it's named Type 76 mod in the Explanation of Evangelion" or something like that until we can confirm that this statement applies to NGE as well.

[]:             Quote Post by Draconis » Sun Jul 17, 2011 11:50 pm Stryker wrote: So, here is what we mostly know: Vehicles CH-53 Super Stallion - UH sounds Type 76 MOD - M1 sounds Humvees - Humvee sounds VTOL Attack Vehicle - Talon sounds, Weapon Sounds: Reisig Sounds VTOL Gunship (featured in EoE) - Shepard sounds, Weapon Sounds - Talon Gunner Sounds Yak-38 Aircraft - Talon Sounds (highspeed) AMX-10 - Bradley Sounds Infantry Arms USP40 Sidearm - Grach sounds G11 Rifle - 416 sounds MPi-9 - M1A1 sounds CZ-75 - 1911 Sounds HPM 1 - Pierra Sounds Glock - M9 sounds Hand Cannon - Rex sounds flame Thrower - Flame thrower sounds Panzerfaust - Carl Sounds Eva weapons Hand Cannon - Rex sounds (deep) Pallet Gun - M8 sounds Hand Bazooka - RPG-7 sounds (tripled, deepened) Sniper Rifle - M95 sounds (Deepened) Positron Sniper Rifle - Rorsch sounds (deepened) I'm sure there are more then just that.

[]:             Quote Post by Stryker » Mon Jul 18, 2011 6:45 am @Ornette: Please do so. @Draconis: Since we're doing a fandub for Eva, we are also bringing alternate weapon sounds for the.. um, weapons. And considering how these are in such high quality, why not? We don't really lose anything from changing them. Sure, the pallet gun sounds like an infantry firearm, but it doesn't; We will edit the sounds of the guns so they sound deeper, more powerful, and all around larger.

[]:             Quote Post by BobBQ » Mon Jul 18, 2011 1:44 pm Coming in a bit late here, but since Ornette brought it to my attention... Stryker wrote: Well, it seems that the Nerv Sentinel seemed to be carrying an Uzi, not a MPi-9.

[]:             Quote Post by Stryker » Mon Jul 18, 2011 1:47 pm I might need to take another look at it, but I'll take your word BobBQ

[]:             Quote Post by Draconis » Mon Jul 18, 2011 3:47 pm Stryker wrote: @Draconis: Since we're doing a fandub for Eva, we are also bringing alternate weapon sounds for the.. um, weapons. And considering how these are in such high quality, why not? We don't really lose anything from changing them. Sure, the pallet gun sounds like an infantry firearm, but it doesn't; We will edit the sounds of the guns so they sound deeper, more powerful, and all around larger.

Multiple Launch Rocket Systems shoot at Sachiel in episode 1, and you see a battalion of M-60 Patton tanks (or a Japanese equivalent from the same generation of AFVs)* lined up to fight aforesaid Angel at the start of episode 1. Hummvees get a lot of play in different parts of the series. I think Nerv guys drive around in Fuchs transport vehicles in Rebuild, but I'm not completely sure. Misato keeps a USP40 as her sidearm. All I can think of off the top of my head, I'll see if I come up with anything else. *The M-60 series became obsolete in the early nineties after Desert Storm. Why does Japan still have them in 2015?

Article: Military Weapons vehicles in Eva
 11, 2011 4:59 am This page can be useful for personal weapons, while this other one is useful for the vehicles. Anyway the later needs some clean-up and it's lacking some vehicles and some infos, for example there's no reference to the "Type-76 mod" mentioned by symbv in the Type 74 section. Actually I really forgot the Explanation of Evangelion (the silly video was subbed in the Italian BD of 1.11 but I never bored watching it), it'll be useful to source the remaining vehicles (together with the CRC 2.22 that I own); I'm going to finish working on it as soon as I can.

Anyway I have a question for symbv: can you confirm that the "Type 76 mod" was already called like that in NGE? Do you remember the source by chance (I'd like to mention it in the wiki)? It's effectively called like that in the "Explanation of Evangelion" but I never heard anything about it before you mentioned it (so I'm not sure if it applies to NGE as well, I think that it's the case but I'd like to have a direct confirmation).
